steps.title: Write perimeter using hypotenuse
steps.content:
Let $h$ be the hypotenuse. In an isosceles right triangle, each leg is
$$
\frac{h}{\sqrt{2}}
$$
So the perimeter is
$$
h+2(\frac{h}{\sqrt{2}})=h+h\sqrt{2}=h(1+\sqrt{2})
$$

steps.title: Match the given perimeter
steps.content: Set this equal to the given perimeter and solve. $$ h(1+\sqrt{2})=20+20\sqrt{2} $$ $$ h(1+\sqrt{2})=20(1+\sqrt{2}) $$ $$ h=20 $$ The answer is C.
